subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Ancient Greek Astronomy
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Ancient Greek Astronomy.

hook.text: Ancient Greeks didn't just watch the sky—they tried to explain it. They asked why the cosmos works as it does.

buildup.text: Aristotle argued Earth was the center of the universe. Ptolemy built a detailed model that lasted 1,400 years.

discovery.text: Aristarchus proposed the Sun was at the center around 250 BC. He was right—but nobody believed him for 1,800 years.

twist.text: Eratosthenes measured Earth's circumference using shadows and geometry. He was accurate to within 2% of the true value.

climax.text: Greek astronomy combined observation with reasoning. This shift from recording to explaining changed science forever.

punchline.text: From watching to wondering why. The Greeks invented scientific astronomy.

quiz.question: Who first proposed that the Sun, not Earth, was at the center?
